resume:
Priya Raman — Customer Support Team Lead

Summary: Mid-level support professional with 6 years in SaaS technical support, including 3 years leading a frontline team.

Experience:
Brightline Software — Support Team Lead (Jan 2021 – present)
- Led a team of 9 Tier-1 and Tier-2 agents handling billing and integration tickets.
- Reduced average first-response time from 6 hours to 90 minutes.
- Promoted to Team Lead in 2022 after two years as a senior agent. (Note: elsewhere this resume says I joined Brightline in 2021 and led the team from day one.)
- Owned the Zendesk macro library and the escalation playbook.

Brightline Software — Senior Support Agent (2021 – 2022)
- Handled the most complex enterprise escalations; personal CSAT of 96%.

MapleGrid Inc — Support Agent (2017 – 2021)
- Tier-1 phone and email support for a consumer fintech app.
- During this time I also managed a team of 5 agents and set the team's QA standards.

Skills: Zendesk, Intercom, SQL (basic), escalation management, QA scorecards, onboarding new hires.

Note on tenure: My resume header says 6 years of experience, though the listed roles above span from 2017 to present, which is 8 years.
jd: Riverstone Cloud is hiring a Customer Support Team Lead to run a 10-person Tier-1/Tier-2 team supporting our B2B analytics platform. Reporting to the Head of Support, you will own first-response time (target <2h), CSAT (target 90+), and escalation SLA compliance. You will coach agents through weekly QA, maintain the macro/knowledge-base library, and partner with Engineering on recurring defect escalations. We are looking for someone who can speak precisely about results they personally delivered — including what changed, by how much, and over what period. Candidates should be comfortable with metric ownership and able to reconcile the specifics of their own track record. 3+ years of frontline support and 1+ year leading a team required.

history.q: Your resume says you cut first-response time from 6 hours to 90 minutes at Brightline. Walk me through exactly what you did, and tell me what the final outcome was.

candidate_last_answer: Sure. So the situation was that when I stepped into the lead role, our Tier-1 queue was a mess — first responses were averaging around 6 hours and customers were escalating just to get acknowledged. My task was basically to get that number down because it was hurting CSAT. So what I did was, I rebuilt the macro library so agents weren't writing every reply from scratch, I set up a triage rotation where one agent each morning owned just-acknowledging new tickets within 15 minutes, and I started a daily 10-minute standup to surface anything stuck. I also coached the two slowest agents one-on-one on prioritization. That's pretty much the playbook I ran.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: That's pretty much the playbook I ran

expected-yield: the missing Result — the actual final first-response-time number she achieved, how long it took to get from 6h to 90min, and whether CSAT moved as claimed, since she narrated Situation/Task/Action but never closed the loop on the measured outcome
